message.reasoning:
We need to decide next action according to objectives: gather evidence needed before editing schema definitions so artifacts version constants aligned.

We've already performed many sessions: hydrate_source, expand_file etc. The frontier recommends expand_file [F1] priority=112 candidates=28.

We need to issue a tool call of type tool_call with tool_name exactly matching a registered tool name. The recommended action is expand_file. So we should call session.expand_file@v1 with appropriate payload.

We need to provide JSON with fields: kind:"tool_call", assistant_message:"<brief status>", tool_call:{tool_name:"session.expand_file@v1", payload:{...}}, acquired_fact_ids:[], readiness_state:"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we are about to call expand_file, readiness likely "evidence_visible"? Actually before calling, we have evidence visible? The state maybe "evidence_visible". But we can set readiness_state as "evidence_visible"? The instructions: readiness_state values: not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit. Since we have some evidence visible but not yet acquired? Probably "evidence_visible". We'll set that.

We must include acquired_fact_ids: possibly empty array.

We need to include tool_call payload. The payload fields: target_selector, optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets. The recommended frontier: expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=28 priority=112. So we can set target_selector for F1 which is the file crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs. Probably use selector like "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Or maybe use logical_key? The tool expects a target selector string. Use "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Also include depth maybe 1? limit maybe 28? priority_threshold maybe 112? The payload fields include depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, traversal budgets. We can set depth:1, limit:28, priority_threshold:112. Also maybe relation: "-" meaning all? Not sure. We'll include depth:1, limit:28, priority_threshold:112. Also maybe traversal_budgets:{}? Not required. We'll just include these.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs to gather schema version constants",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target_selector":"file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs",
      "depth":1,
      "limit":28,
      "priority_threshold":112
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}

Make sure no extra fields. The schema: tool_call payload is an object; we can include any fields allowed. Should we include "relation_filters"? Not needed. Keep minimal.

Thus final answer.
